turns.user_message: Honestly, I'm feeling a bit overwhelmed. I've been trying to quit for a while now, and it's just so hard. I've been smoking for 15 years, and I've tried quitting twice before. I'm worried that I'll never be able to stay quit.
step2_rag_chunks: If you have tried to quit before, that experience is actually useful information â€” not a failure. What worked even a little? What made it harder? Most people who successfully quit have tried several times before it sticks. Each attempt teaches you something about your own pattern.

It is completely normal to want to quit and also feel unsure about it at the same time. Most people feel this way â€” one part of them wants to stop, another part is not ready. That conflict is not weakness, it is just how change works. It helps to talk about both sides openly rather than pushing the doubts aside.
Relapse can happen during the quitting process. Instead of feeling discouraged, treat it as a learning experience and continue trying to quit.

step4_session_phase.phase_instruction: You are in the PLANNING phase. You have enough understanding of this patient. You can now gently introduce ONE relevant coping strategy or technique based on their specific triggers and past attempts. Keep it simple — one idea at a time, check if they are open to it.

Therapist: Hello, Rachel. Welcome to EDosthi. I'm here to help you with tobacco cessation. Would you like to tell me about your tobacco use and what brings you here today?

Patient: Honestly, I'm feeling a bit overwhelmed. I've been trying to quit for a while now, and it's just so hard. I've been smoking for 15 years, and I've tried quitting twice before. I'm worried that I'll never be able to stay quit.

Therapist: I can imagine how tough it must be for you. It takes a lot of courage to keep trying. Can you tell me more about what's been making it hard for you to stay quit? What are some of the challenges you've faced, and how have you been feeling about your previous quit attempts?

Patient: Well, I have depression and anxiety, and I'm worried that quitting smoking will make them worse. I've experienced increased anxiety and irritability during previous quit attempts.
